page: Amazon Web Services m6g.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processors. It features 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The instance is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. No local NVMe storage or dedicated GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core performance and memory efficiency. The instance achieves a strong rating in multi-core CPU stress tests with a stress-ng score of 97,020.81 and cached small-block read memory bandwidth of 3,782.99 GB/sec. It also demonstrates high throughput in software compilation with a Geekbench clang score of 43,442.0, Redis SET operations at 72.59 million operations per second, and static web serving at 45.98 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by lower scores in single-threaded gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core tests. Large-block uncached memory operations and multi-threaded decompression fall within the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and strong multi-core throughput relative to its hardware class, making it a viable alternative to traditional x86 instances for parallelizable workloads. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a tradeoff for I/O-heavy applications. This instance is suited for multi-threaded applications, containerized microservices, Redis caching layers, high-throughput web serving, and software build pipelines. It is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM inference, where its performance scales down.
description: Amazon Web Services m6g.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by 64 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 256 GB of system memory, a 25 Gbps baseline network interface, and no local SSD storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks highlight strong multi-core capabilities, with exceptional results in software compilation, Redis operations, and static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance and large-scale LLM inference speeds are weaker. The instance offers a balanced resource profile and high multi-core density, making it a cost-efficient choice for parallelized workloads. It is suited for containerized microservices, multi-threaded applications, caching layers, and CI/CD build pipelines.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.16xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 instance featuring 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB of RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6g.16xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core ARM64 instance delivering balanced memory and high multi-core throughput for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 cores at 2.5 GHz
Features 256 GB of system memory with a balanced 4 GB per core ratio
Delivers 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong benchmark performance in Redis operations and software compilation
Suited for containerized microservices, web serving, and CI/CD pipelines
Lacks local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware
